title: broken_authentication
description: Weak session management and predictable session tokens allow an attacker to enumerate valid session IDs and take over active user accounts.

timeline.title: Account Takeover Reports
timeline.description: Multiple users report being unexpectedly logged out, with session activity from unfamiliar IPs appearing in their account history.

timeline.title: Session Enumeration Detected
timeline.description: WAF logs reveal sequential session token enumeration attempts. Tokens appear to be numerically sequential, making brute force feasible.

timeline.title: Accounts Compromised
timeline.description: At least 23 active user sessions have been hijacked. The attacker is accessing account data and making purchases using victim payment methods.

timeline.title: All Sessions Invalidated
timeline.description: All active sessions invalidated globally. Session token algorithm replaced with cryptographically random 128-bit tokens.

timeline.title: MFA Enforcement Initiated
timeline.description: MFA enforced for all accounts. Anomalous session detection implemented to flag logins from new IPs or devices.

timeline.title: Security Audit Complete
timeline.description: Full authentication flow audit completed. Penetration test validates session token randomness. Incident closed with lessons learned.

node-1.title: Session Hijacking via Predictable Tokens
node-1.situation: Users are reporting account takeover. WAF logs show an IP address making thousands of requests with incrementing session IDs (e.g., sess_10001, sess_10002...). Your session tokens are sequentially generated and easily enumerable. Active hijacking of 23 accounts is confirmed.
node-1.question: What is your immediate action?

options.label: A
options.text: Invalidate all active sessions globally, block the attacking IP, and immediately replace the session token generation algorithm with cryptographically random tokens.

options.label: B
options.text: Rate-limit session validation requests to slow the enumeration without invalidating existing sessions.

options.label: C
options.text: Add a CAPTCHA to the login page to prevent the automated enumeration.

node-2.title: Assess Account Takeover Damage
node-2.situation: 23 accounts were compromised. The attacker accessed personal data and made unauthorised purchases using saved payment methods. Affected users need to be notified and damages assessed.
node-2.question: How do you manage affected users?

options.label: A
options.text: Notify all 23 affected users, reverse fraudulent transactions, force password resets, and report the breach to the payment processor and relevant regulators.

options.label: B
options.text: Only reverse the fraudulent transactions and notify users to change passwords without regulatory notification.

node-3.title: Fix the Session Management Vulnerability
node-3.situation: The root cause is sequential session ID generation. The fix of cryptographically random tokens is deployed. However, the authentication system also lacks account lockout, MFA, and session binding controls.
node-3.question: What additional authentication controls do you implement?

options.label: A
options.text: Enforce MFA for all accounts, implement session binding to user-agent and IP, add account lockout after repeated failed session validation attempts.

options.label: B
options.text: Only the random session token fix is sufficient — no further authentication changes are needed.

node-4.title: Validate the Authentication Hardening
node-4.situation: Authentication controls have been updated. Before closing the incident, you need to validate that the new controls are effective and no other authentication weaknesses exist.
node-4.question: How do you validate the fixes?

options.label: A
options.text: Commission a penetration test focused on authentication and session management, run automated DAST scans against all auth endpoints, and monitor for further enumeration attempts.

options.label: B
options.text: Internal testing by the development team is sufficient — no external validation is needed.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: Session enumeration and account takeover were contained. All sessions were invalidated, random tokens deployed, MFA enforced, and a penetration test validated the hardened authentication system. Affected users were notified and regulatory obligations met.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
